assertions.title: Valid roadmap deep links restore the intended node-focused graph state

steps.expected: Roadmap graph focus restores with the BETTER Terminal node visibly selected
steps.observed: The page opened in the Roadmap graph state with Product Evolution expanded, BETTER Terminal selected, and the roadmap detail panel visible.

steps.expected: The same node-focused roadmap state survives refresh
steps.observed: After refresh the URL remained /#node-pe-terminal-beta and the BETTER Terminal detail panel reopened in the same roadmap context.

evidence.consoleErrors: none; only React DevTools/HMR info logs were emitted.
evidence.network: No failing requests observed; the hash-backed deep-link flow did not surface any network errors in agent-browser.
notes: The refreshed state kept the same hash and reopened the selected roadmap node.
Captured body text and DOM checks confirmed the selected node remained BETTER Terminal (Closed Beta).

assertions.title: Invalid roadmap deep links fall back to a coherent node-focused graph state

steps.expected: A coherent fallback appears instead of a blank or broken roadmap surface
steps.observed: The roadmap showed a visible invalid-link fallback banner with a Reset & Explore Roadmap recovery action while leaving the atlas usable.

steps.expected: The stale hash is cleared and the user returns to a coherent default roadmap state
steps.observed: Clicking Reset & Explore Roadmap cleared the URL back to / and returned the user to the normal roadmap overview.

evidence.network: No failing requests observed; agent-browser showed only the expected local document, font, image, stylesheet, and script requests for the fallback load.

assertions.title: Mobile roadmap exploration remains node-first and recoverable

steps.action: Switch to iPhone 14 emulation and open /#graph-roadmap
steps.expected: The roadmap surface remains usable on a narrow viewport
steps.observed: The mobile roadmap rendered readable copy, visible related-node controls, and a recoverable roadmap focus state without clipped or unusable controls.

steps.action: Expand Product Evolution and open BETTER Terminal (Closed Beta)
steps.expected: Parent/child relationships and active node state remain clear on mobile
steps.observed: The branch expanded, the BETTER Terminal node became pressed/active, and its detail panel opened with readable copy.

steps.action: Use Back to overview in the focused roadmap state
steps.expected: Users can recover without getting stranded in the detail state
steps.observed: The back control returned to overview successfully during the recorded mobile flow.

evidence.network: No failing requests observed during the mobile flow.
notes: DOM state during the active mobile step showed the roadmap surface and BETTER Terminal node both in pressed state.
The mobile flow remained touch-usable and recoverable through the Back to overview action.

assertions.title: Users can traverse directly between nodes while retaining visible graph context

steps.action: Open /#graph-roadmap and use the related-node control to move to Tokenomics
steps.expected: Users can move directly between nodes while the graph context remains visibly available
steps.observed: The related-node control switched focus directly from Roadmap to Tokenomics and updated the URL to #graph-tokenomics.

steps.expected: The surrounding graph shell remains visibly present during the transition, not just document-style content
steps.observed: After the transition, the focused Tokenomics panel filled the viewport and the graph overview/map was scrolled fully off-screen; only the related-node pills remained visible at the top of the panel.

evidence.network: No failing requests observed during node-to-node traversal.

assertions.issues: Direct traversal works, but the visible graph map/context does not stay on screen after the transition; the experience collapses into the destination content panel rather than retaining clearly visible surrounding graph context.

frictions.description: Graph-shell persistence carried the previously selected roadmap detail into later mobile runs, so opening #graph-roadmap on mobile could land in an already-selected detail state instead of a neutral overview.
frictions.resolution: Used the in-product Back to overview control and then re-ran the mobile interaction flow from the branch list.

frictions.description: agent-browser request logging was sparse for hash-only state restoration, so direct deep-link refresh evidence depended more on screenshots, the recording, and DOM state than on a rich request trace.
frictions.resolution: Captured before/after-refresh screenshots, a refresh recording, and DOM/body-state evidence for the valid deep-link flow.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 3 passed (VAL-ROADMAP-006, VAL-ROADMAP-007, VAL-ROADMAP-009) and 1 failed (VAL-ROADMAP-012, because direct traversal hides the graph overview and leaves only destination content visible).
